lingam.LinGAM#

class lingam.LinGAM(n_splines=10, lam=1.0, spline_order=3, constraint=None)[source]#

Penalised B-spline GAM for a single smooth term y = f(x) + eps.

Parameters:

  • n_splines (int) – Number of B-spline basis functions K. Must satisfy K > spline_order.

  • lam (float) – Smoothing parameter (lambda >= 0).

  • spline_order (int) – B-spline order (polynomial degree).

  • constraint (Optional[str]) – Shape constraint for the smooth term. One of None, 'mono_inc', 'mono_dec', 'convex', 'concave', 'periodic'.

__init__(n_splines=10, lam=1.0, spline_order=3, constraint=None)[source]#

Fraction of null deviance explained (pseudo R-squared).

Fit the model with current hyper-parameters.

Select optimal (lam, n_splines) by minimising GCV.

Gaussian log-likelihood.

prediction_intervals(x[, width])

Attributes

is_fitted

n_coefs

Effective number of spline coefficients (reduced for periodic).
property n_coefs: int#

Effective number of spline coefficients (reduced for periodic).

property is_fitted: bool#
gridsearch(x, y, lam=None, n_splines=None, gamma=1.4, robust=False)[source]#

Select optimal (lam, n_splines) by minimising GCV.

Always uses the fast QR+Cholesky path for standard fits. When robust=True, uses Huber IRLS within each candidate evaluation.

Return type:

LinGAM

fit(x, y, robust=False)[source]#

Fit the model with current hyper-parameters.

Return type:
